skill.name: implement
skill.description: Guides an AI coding agent to implement work from a PRD or issue list with tests, review, and a final commit.
skill.summary: Implement PRD or issue work with test-driven checkpoints and review.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 7. Both are false positives in plain workflow prose, with no cryptographic implementation, no command execution, and no data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is a natural-language description of implementation work. No weak cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only metadata prose. There is no code, algorithm selection, cryptographic library use, or security-sensitive operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 7, but the line only instructs the assistant to implement user-described work. No weak cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain task guidance. It contains no executable content, crypto references, or hidden instruction pattern.

content.value_statement: Turning a PRD or issue list into working code requires planning, tests, review, and a clean finish. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a direct workflow for implementation work.
actual_capabilities: Directs an AI coding agent to implement work described in a PRD or issue list.
Encourages test-driven development when suitable project seams are agreed.
Prompts regular typechecking and focused test runs during implementation.
Requires a full test suite run at the end of the work.
Adds a final review step before completion.
Instructs the agent to commit completed work to the current branch.
limitations: It does not define project-specific build, test, or lint commands.
It depends on the user providing a clear PRD, issue list, or implementation goal.
It does not include deployment, release, or rollback instructions.
It may need adaptation for teams that do not want automatic commits.

use_cases.title: Build a feature from a PRD
use_cases.description: Use the skill to turn a product requirement into code, tests, review, and a final commit.

use_cases.title: Resolve a focused issue list
use_cases.description: Use the skill to handle a small set of bugs or tasks with regular validation.

use_cases.title: Standardize agent implementation flow
use_cases.description: Use the skill to make AI-assisted implementation follow testing and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a small issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implement skill to complete this issue. Run focused tests and typechecking before you finish.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task with one clear issue

prompt_templates.title: Implement from a PRD
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implement skill to build the attached PRD. Identify test seams, implement the work, review it, and commit the result.

prompt_templates.title: Implement multiple related fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implement skill to complete these related issues. Keep changes scoped, validate each area, then run the full suite.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a cross-module feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implement skill for this cross-module PRD. Plan the sequence, use TDD where practical, run regular checks, review the result, and commit.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced implementation across several code areas

output_examples.input: Implement the issue that adds validation to the checkout form.
output_examples.output: The checkout validation is implemented, focused tests pass, typechecking passes, and the completed work is committed to the current branch.

output_examples.input: Build the PRD for saved search filters.
output: Implementation completed against the PRD scope.
Relevant tests were added or updated.
A final review was performed before commit.

output_examples.input: Complete these three bug fixes in the current repository.
output_examples.output: The fixes are implemented with regular validation, the full test suite has been run, and the branch contains a final commit.

best_practices: Provide a clear PRD or issue list before invoking the skill.
Agree on test seams before expecting test-driven implementation.
Tell the agent whether committing to the current branch is allowed.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when the work scope is vague or exploratory.
Do not skip project-specific test and typecheck instructions.
Do not use automatic commits when the repository workflow forbids them.

faq.question: What does this skill help implement?
faq.answer: It helps implement work described by a PRD, issue list, or clear user request.

faq.question: Does it write project-specific tests automatically?
faq.answer: It tells the agent to use tests, but the exact tests depend on the repository.

faq.question: Can it run the full test suite?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the agent to run the full suite once near the end.

faq.question: Does it require TDD?
faq.answer: It recommends TDD where practical and where suitable seams are agreed.

faq.question: Will it commit changes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the agent to commit completed work to the current branch.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains workflow guidance only, with no confirmed dangerous behavior.
